# Mailient

> Mailient removes email from your to-do list entirely. It is an autonomous AI inbox employee for solo founders: it reads every email, prioritizes what matters, drafts replies in the founder's own voice, books meetings, and follows up automatically — running on schedule while the founder sleeps. Each morning the founder wakes up to one short briefing instead of an inbox.

## What Mailient is

- Mailient is an AI email product that works on top of an existing Gmail account (secure Google OAuth; emails stay in Gmail).
- Unlike Superhuman, Shortwave, Fyxer, or Gmail's built-in AI — which make people *faster* at email — Mailient does the email work itself, autonomously, including overnight scheduled runs with no tab open.
- The core promise: "Wake up to an inbox that's already handled." The user reviews a short morning briefing and approves the few decisions that genuinely need a human.
- Nothing is sent without the user's approval by default. Autonomous sending exists only as an explicit per-agent opt-in.

## Who it is for

- Solo founders who run their company out of one Gmail account and are drowning in it. One founder, one Gmail, no team seats (multi-seat is on the roadmap).

## Key capabilities (plain names)

- Triage: reads everything; surfaces only the emails that deserve attention (deals, decisions, real requests). Newsletters and noise are handled silently. Internally called "Sift."
- Drafts: replies that sound like the user, learned from their last 90 days of sent mail. Drafts wait in Gmail for one-click approval.
- The agent: reads threads, books meetings against real calendar availability, chases follow-ups, and runs scheduled background jobs (e.g., a 7am inbox sweep, a Friday digest, meeting prep). Internally called "Arcus."
- Morning briefing: one email each morning summarizing what was processed, drafted, booked — and the few items that need the founder.
- Integrations: Gmail, Google Calendar, Notion, Slack, Cal.com.

## Pricing (single plan, everything included)

- Monthly: $29/month (3-day free trial).
- Annual: $199/year.
- Lifetime Founder: $499 one-time.
- All tiers include the full product with unlimited use; they differ only in how you pay. No free tier. No hidden API or overage fees.

## Security & privacy (verifiable claims only)

- Emails are encrypted in the user's browser (AES-256-GCM) before reaching Mailient's servers; decryption keys never leave the browser.
- Personal data is stripped before any AI processes content.
- Email content is never used to train AI models — not Mailient's, not its AI providers', not anyone's.
- Access is standard Google OAuth and can be revoked anytime at myaccount.google.com/permissions.

## Company

- Built by Maulik, a 14-year-old solo founder.
- Website: https://mailient.xyz

- [Home](https://mailient.xyz/): what Mailient does, the morning brief, FAQs

- [Arcus](https://mailient.xyz/product/arcus): the AI that runs the inbox
- [Sift](https://mailient.xyz/product/sift): how prioritization decides what needs you
- [Drafts](https://mailient.xyz/product/drafts): replies in your voice
- [Security](https://mailient.xyz/security): encryption and data-handling architecture
- [Blog](https://mailient.xyz/blogs): essays on AI email agents, triage, voice, and encryption
